msgs.text:
Turn 118 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed The required current-turn attachments are present, and I used only the files attached to this message. The problem statement’s target is still the over-G question: whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓH such that πH∘Φ=πS. The problem file defines ΓS,ΓH, the canonical quotient maps to G, and this exact over-G isomorphism condition.

The Turn 118 state is unchanged from Turn 117:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate remains closed. The attached sweep records the stronger F=P⋊G-level statement: there is a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F such thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,i.e.ΓS≅GΓH.

This is exactly the requested condition πH∘Φ=πS.No structural computation should be reopened under the current inputs. The frozen proof chain remains:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+ residual restricted-Lie induction+ normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+ Certificates D.1–D.4+ finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The history explicitly records that the non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled, the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes by the Cm=Lm induction, the coefficient tower closes after exact normalization to (2,t) and the Koszul--Witt cascade, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness globalizes the finite solutions to the over-G automorphism.The compactness mechanism is still the corrected filtered-Nielsen one: for coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J, the finite sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms. Certificates A–E prove SN,J=∅; compactness gives a compatible inverse limit; the limit yields an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F; and intersecting all PN,J gives Φ(rS−1)=rH.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments contain only mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md; they do not contain object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs independent of the history ledger. Therefore the strict gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. Ledger-recorded verifier claims support the theorem coordinate, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict Certificate D gate.

So the attachment-only transition rule applies again:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 118 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 117. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN118-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
